food_subgroup: Poultry offal — deep-fried or grilled skewered chicken gizzard

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a chicken gizzard food product class (FOODON_03315595); no specific entry for crispy/skewered preparation exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers chicken gizzard as an ingredient category; no skewered or fried preparation sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226934003 covers chicken gizzard as a food concept; no specific preparation variant for crispy skewered form exists.

allergens: gluten (from soy sauce and cornstarch coating)

serving.description: 5 skewers (approximately 5 gizzard pieces per skewer or 1 gizzard per skewer)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is 5 skewers of crispy chicken gizzard, estimated at approximately 150 g total, representing a typical street-food or hawker portion as sold at Singaporean Chinese food stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken gizzard, all classes, cooked, simmered — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05178); adapted with frying adjustment for crispy preparation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked chicken gizzard (NDB 05178 / FDC ID 171477). Values adjusted upward for fat and energy to account for deep-frying in oil and cornstarch coating typical of crispy skewered preparation. Protein adjusted slightly downward to reflect dilution from coating. Sodium adjusted upward to reflect seasoning (soy sauce, salt). This is an estimated adaptation; no exact HPB Singapore or USDA Branded Food entry for crispy chicken gizzard skewers was identified.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed protein-fat-carbohydrate foods with cornstarch coating — analogous estimate based on fried chicken products; University of Sydney GI Database reference for fried battered chicken
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for crispy chicken gizzard skewers. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on analogy with fried battered chicken products listed in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 40–50 for protein-dominant fried foods with starch coatings. The moderate protein and fat content suppresses glycemic response relative to pure starch foods. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 12.75) / 100 ≈ 5.7, rounded to 5.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce seasoning and salt (~480 mg sodium per 100 g; ~720 mg per 150 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid crispy chicken gizzard skewers due to the high sodium load from soy sauce and salt seasoning, which can acutely elevate blood pressure; if consumed, portion should be restricted to 1–2 skewers and paired with low-sodium foods for the remainder of the meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate from cornstarch coating with low GI (~45); high fat content from deep-frying may impair insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycemic index (~45) of crispy chicken gizzard skewers means blood glucose rise is relatively modest, but the high fat content from deep-frying (~12 g fat per 100 g) may contribute to insulin resistance with regular consumption; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion to 3–4 skewers and prefer grilled over deep-fried variants where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High cholesterol content (~185 mg per 100 g), elevated sodium, saturated fat from frying, and trans fat from frying oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Crispy chicken gizzard skewers are high in dietary cholesterol, saturated fat, and sodium, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with existing CVD or dyslipidaemia should consume this food only occasionally and in small portions, and should prefer steamed or boiled gizzard preparations to reduce fat and cholesterol load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy chicken gizzard skewers (5 pieces) is a popular Chinese street-food and hawker dish consisting of chicken gizzards — the muscular stomach organ of the chicken — that are marinated in soy sauce, garlic, ginger, five-spice powder, and pepper, coated in cornstarch, and then deep-fried until golden and crispy before being threaded onto bamboo skewers. The dish is commonly sold at Chinese hawker stalls and night markets across Singapore, China, and Southeast Asia. The texture is characteristically chewy on the inside with a thin, crunchy exterior shell from the cornstarch coating and hot oil. The flavour profile is savoury, umami-rich, and mildly spiced, with the deep-frying imparting a roasted, slightly caramelised note. Nutritionally, chicken gizzard is a lean organ meat that is high in protein (~18.5 g per 100 g), iron (~2.8 mg per 100 g), zinc (~2.5 mg per 100 g), and vitamin B12 (~0.9 mcg per 100 g), making it a micronutrient-dense food. However, the deep-frying process significantly increases fat content (~12 g per 100 g) and caloric density (~218 kcal per 100 g) compared to boiled gizzard. The high cholesterol content (~185 mg per 100 g) and elevated sodium (~480 mg per 100 g) from seasoning make this dish a concern for individuals with cardiovascular disease or hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low category), reflecting the dominant protein-fat matrix with only a modest cornstarch carbohydrate contribution. A standard serving of 5 skewers weighs approximately 150 g and provides roughly 327 kcal. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten (due to soy sauce) or high-cholesterol foods.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for crispy chicken gizzard skewers was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cooked chicken gizzard data (FDC ID 171477) with adjustments for deep-frying and seasoning. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using fried battered chicken as the reference food. Confidence is medium due to the adaptation and estimation required.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477, cooked chicken gizzard) with upward adjustments for fat (+5 g/100g), energy, sodium, and carbohydrate to reflect deep-frying in oil and cornstarch coating. These are estimates and should be replaced with direct laboratory analysis or an exact branded product entry if available. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked chicken gizzard (FDC ID 171477). 3. vitamin_e_mg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked chicken gizzard (FDC ID 171477). 4. Halal status is marked false because the restaurant (Lan Ting Xu Lanzhou Beef Noodles) is not confirmed halal-certified; consumers should verify with the specific vendor. 5. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous fried battered chicken products from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for chicken gizzard skewers was identified in peer-reviewed literature. 6. Serving size of 150 g (5 skewers) is an estimate based on typical hawker portion sizes; actual weight may vary by vendor. 7. The cuisine_tags in the input JSON include 'Drinks & Beverages' and 'Noodles' which appear to be restaurant-level tags for Lan Ting Xu rather than food-level tags for this dish; these have been disregarded for classification purposes.
